title: Magic Strings
description:
Consider the sequence of strings F1, F2, . . . , defined as:

F1 = ab,

Fk+1 = FkFkb.

Calculate the number of distinct subsequences of the string Fn modulo 109 + 7.
input:
The first line of input contains a single integer t (1 ≤ t ≤ 10), which is the number of test cases.

The second line of input contains t integers n (1 ≤ n ≤ 1018), one for each test case.
output: For each test case, output the single integer which is the answer to the problem. Separate consecutive answers by single spaces.
hint: The first three strings are: F1 = ab, F2 = ababb, and F3 = ababbababbb.
source: Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Summer 2019, Day 4: Oleksandr Kulkov Contest 2
